items.definition_en: A train is a set of connected vehicles that run on tracks.
items.example_en: We took the train to the city.

items.definition_en: A railway is a system of tracks for trains.
items.example_en: The railway connects many towns.

items.definition_en: A station is a place where trains stop for people to get on or off.
items.example_en: She waited for him at the train station.

items.definition_en: A track is the pair of rails where trains run.
items.example_en: The train came down the track.

items.definition_en: A platform is a raised area next to railway tracks for people to board trains.
items.example_en: They waited on the platform for their train.

items.definition_en: A carriage is one of the sections of a train for people to sit in.
items.example_en: We found seats in the last carriage.

items.definition_en: A conductor checks tickets on a train.
items.example_en: The conductor walked down the aisle.

items.definition_en: An engine pulls the train along the tracks.
items.example_en: The engine was at the front of the train.

items.definition_en: A ticket is a piece of paper you need to travel on a train.
items.example_en: He bought a ticket to London.

items.definition_en: A railcard offers a discount on train tickets.
items.example_en: She used her railcard to get a cheaper fare.

items.definition_en: A passenger is a person who travels on a train.
items.example_en: Many passengers boarded the morning train.

items.definition_en: A driver is a person who operates a train.
items.example_en: The driver started the engine.

items.definition_en: A timetable shows the times trains arrive and leave.
items.example_en: He checked the timetable for the next train.

items.definition_en: A signal tells trains when they can move.
items.example_en: The train stopped for a red signal.

items.definition_en: A waiting room is where people sit before their train arrives.
items.example_en: She sat in the waiting room at the station.

items.definition_en: A guard is a person who looks after the train and its safety.
items.example_en: The guard checked the doors before leaving.

items.definition_en: A sleeper is a train carriage with beds for sleeping.
items.example_en: We booked a sleeper for the night trip.

items.definition_en: A rail pass lets you travel on many trains for a set time.
items.example_en: He bought a rail pass for his holiday.

items.definition_en: A turnstile is a gate in a station that only lets one person through at a time.
items.example_en: You must scan your ticket at the turnstile.

items.definition_en: A buffer is a device at the end of a track to stop trains.
items.example_en: The train stopped safely at the buffer.
